classObjective: Graph systems of linear inequalities and identify the overlapping solution region (feasible region).
workedExamples: 7-5, Example 1 — Graph Systems of Inequalities in Slope-Intercept Form
7-5, Example 2 — Graph Systems with Horizontal, Vertical, and Standard-Form Boundaries
conceptDevelopment: Mini-lesson: shading each inequality; intersection of half-planes; bounded vs. unbounded regions
guidedPractice: Teacher-led practice graphing systems of inequalities and identifying feasible regions
independentPractice: Practice problems graphing systems of inequalities and identifying overlapping solution regions
assessment: Exit ticket: graph the system y >= 2x - 1 and y < -x + 3 and identify the feasible region
capReflection: What does the feasible region represent in a system of inequalities?
